content:
Best,
[Your Name]

⸻

Nandi Marrero – “How to Use an AED”

Dear Nandi,

I really enjoyed reading your instructions on how to use an AED. Your steps are clear, concise, and well-structured, making them easy to follow in an emergency situation. The emphasis on safety—such as calling out “CLEAR” before delivering a shock—is especially strong and shows your understanding of proper AED use.

One thing that could enhance your instructions further is the inclusion of estimated time durations for each step. For example, how long does it typically take for the AED to analyze a heart rhythm? How much time should a person wait between shocks before attempting CPR? These small details could help the reader prepare for real-world scenarios.

Additionally, a troubleshooting section would be helpful—what should a person do if the AED doesn’t turn on, or if the pads don’t adhere properly? Including common errors and how to fix them would strengthen your instructions significantly.
context: From document: Workshop-Style Feedback Letters — chunk 2 (chunk 2/7)
